mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and subsequently S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is elevated and housing starts (HOUST) are declining, it indicates a tightening monetary policy successfully cooling an overheating economy, which typically precedes a period of slower growth and negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 0.15 AND HOUST declines > 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the combined signal's Rank IC is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of post-recession recovery, a sustained rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, leading to improved corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: In identified post-recession recovery regimes, INDPRO increases do not lead to positive SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL), erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to expectations of tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining real GDP (GDPC1) for two consecutive quarters and a significant rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) strongly indicates an economic recession, which severely impacts corporate earnings and leads to significant negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for 2 consecutive quarters AND UNRATE rises > 1pp in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of periods meeting the combined recessionary trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low interest rates (FEDFUNDS), an increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals robust construction activity and broader economic confidence, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: In identified low interest rate regimes, positive HOUST growth does not lead to positive SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
